response:
I don’t have access to your meeting notes or systems, so I can’t say which customers were discussed for “Project Sentinel.” If you point me to the meeting/date or paste the minutes, I’ll extract them. Meanwhile, here are quick ways you can find the list yourself and some ready-to-run queries/templates.

What to check
- Calendar invite / meeting notes (Google Calendar/Outlook) for the Project Sentinel meeting.
- Confluence/SharePoint page for the Sentinel epic or launch – check the page’s “Attendees / Notes / Stakeholders” section.
- Slack / Teams channel where the project is discussed (search for “Sentinel” + the meeting date).
- CRM (Salesforce, Dynamics) for opportunities/projects named “Sentinel” or tagged with the project.
- JIRA/Asana/Trello epics and linked customer stories.
- Email thread from the PM or AMs (search subject: “Sentinel”, “Project Sentinel”, “Sentinel kickoff”, etc.).

Example searches / filters
- Salesforce SOQL:
  SELECT Account.Name, Opportunity.Name, Opportunity.OwnerId
  FROM Opportunity
  WHERE Name LIKE '%Sentinel%' OR Project__c = 'Sentinel'
- Google Drive/Docs: search for: "Sentinel" AND (kickoff OR meeting OR customer OR pilot)
- Outlook/Exchange: subject:(Sentinel) OR body:(Project Sentinel) with date range filter
- Slack: in: #project-sentinel "Sentinel" before:YYYY-MM-DD after:YYYY-MM-DD

If you want to ask the project team quickly
Use this email/Slack template to the PMs or AEs:
- Subject: Quick: which customers were discussed for Project Sentinel?
- Body: Hi team — I’m compiling the customer list for Project Sentinel. Which customer names were discussed (pilot/reference/target)? Please reply with names and status (interested/pilot/reference). Thanks — [Your name]

If you share the meeting date, owner, or paste notes here I’ll extract and format the customer list for you.
